headline: Maya Rudolph Broadway Gambit Sparks Box Office Magic for 'Oh, Mary!'
lede: Comedy queen Maya Rudolph has jolted the Broadway landscape with her electric debut, sending ticket sales for 'Oh, Mary!' soaring to unprecedented heights.
body:
Broadway's theatrical ecosystem just experienced a seismic shift with Maya Rudolph's unexpected stage debut in the quirky comedy 'Oh, Mary!'. The breakout performance has transformed the two-year-old production from a modest theatrical experiment into a must-see springtime sensation.

Rudolph's arrival triggered an immediate and dramatic box office surge, with weekly grosses rocketing to $1.54 million — a remarkable achievement for an off-Broadway production. Her comedic prowess, honed through years of sketch comedy and film roles, translates seamlessly to the live stage.

The show's creative team strategically positioned Rudolph as a transformative headliner, betting on her star power to reinvigorate the production. This calculated risk appears to have paid substantial dividends, proving that the right casting can completely recalibrate a production's momentum.

Industry observers are now watching closely to see whether Rudolph's Broadway breakthrough represents a potential new model for revitalizing theatrical properties. Her crossover appeal from television and film to live performance suggests a broader trend of high-profile entertainers seeking stage credibility.
key_facts: Rudolph boosted 'Oh, Mary!' weekly gross to $1.54 million
First Broadway performance for the comedy star
Production originally launched two years ago
Box office surge signals potential new audience engagement strategy
